name: Acute respiratory distress syndrome (ARDS)
description: Acute respiratory distress syndrome (ARDS) — acute, severe, diffuse lung injury causing hypoxaemic respiratory failure.

en.name: Acute respiratory distress syndrome (ARDS)
en.shortDescription: Acute respiratory distress syndrome (ARDS) — acute, severe, diffuse lung injury causing hypoxaemic respiratory failure.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Acute respiratory distress syndrome (ARDS) is acute, diffuse inflammatory lung injury causing severe hypoxaemic respiratory failure with bilateral pulmonary infiltrates not fully explained by cardiac failure. It is a defining condition of intensive care, typically requiring mechanical ventilation, and is triggered by pneumonia, sepsis, aspiration, trauma and other insults.

## Scope

The set is anchored on the SNOMED concept *4195694 / 67782005 — Acute respiratory distress syndrome*. It captures the diagnosis of ARDS as an acute critical-illness condition.

## Mapping Notes

- ARDS is an acute syndrome, distinct from chronic respiratory comorbidities such as COPD.
- The older term "adult respiratory distress syndrome" refers to the same condition; the current term is "acute respiratory distress syndrome".

fr.name: Syndrome de détresse respiratoire aiguë (SDRA)
fr.shortDescription: Syndrome de détresse respiratoire aiguë (SDRA) — atteinte pulmonaire aiguë, sévère et diffuse, responsable d'une insuffisance respiratoire hypoxémique.
